functional_limitations: Difficulty ambulating distances greater than 200 feet

denial_risk_flags.label: Imaging Not Yet Complete
denial_risk_flags.explanation: X-ray was ordered at today's visit (01/30/2025) with results pending, and MRI has not yet been ordered. Payers require completed imaging results before authorizing surgical procedures such as CPT 27130.
denial_risk_flags.recommendation: Do not submit until X-ray results are available and documented. Consider ordering and completing MRI as well, as United Healthcare typically requires cross-sectional imaging to confirm severity of osteoarthritis prior to authorizing total hip arthroplasty.

denial_risk_flags.label: Insufficient Conservative Care
denial_risk_flags.explanation: Only 1 treatment (OTC pain medication) is documented with no duration, and no formal physical therapy is documented. Payers for CPT 27130 typically require documented failure of physical therapy (minimum 6 weeks), NSAIDs, and at least one injection before approving surgical intervention.
denial_risk_flags.recommendation: Obtain records documenting additional conservative treatments or initiate and document further conservative care before submission. At minimum, document a formal physical therapy course with duration, a named NSAID trial with duration, and consider documenting any injection history.

denial_risk_flags.label: No Physical Therapy Documented
denial_risk_flags.explanation: The referral explicitly states 'No formal physical therapy documented in referral.' United Healthcare and most major payers require documented failure of supervised physical therapy (typically 6–8 weeks minimum) as a prerequisite for total hip arthroplasty authorization.
denial_risk_flags.recommendation: Initiate and document a formal physical therapy program of at least 6–8 weeks duration, or obtain prior PT records if therapy was completed elsewhere, before submitting the prior authorization request.

denial_risk_flags.label: OTC Medication — No Duration or Name
denial_risk_flags.explanation: The only documented conservative treatment is described vaguely as 'over-the-counter pain medication at home' with no specific drug name, dosage, or duration. Payers require specific NSAID names and treatment duration to evaluate adequacy of conservative care.
denial_risk_flags.recommendation: Addend the chart to specify the exact medication(s) used (e.g., ibuprofen, naproxen), dosage, frequency, and duration of use, as well as the reason for discontinuation or failure.

denial_risk_flags.label: Preliminary Chart — Pre-op Workup Incomplete
denial_risk_flags.explanation: The chart explicitly states 'Chart is preliminary referral documentation. Full pre-op workup pending.' Submitting a prior authorization based on incomplete documentation significantly increases denial risk.
denial_risk_flags.recommendation: Delay PA submission until the full pre-operative workup is complete, imaging results are available, and a comprehensive H&P with ASA classification is documented.

denial_risk_flags.label: No ASA Classification Documented
denial_risk_flags.explanation: No ASA physical status classification is documented. For a surgical procedure like total hip arthroplasty (CPT 27130), payers and anesthesia reviewers expect ASA classification to be present in pre-operative documentation.
denial_risk_flags.recommendation: Document ASA classification in the pre-operative assessment prior to PA submission.

denial_risk_flags.label: Symptom Duration Vague — No Specific Timeframe
denial_risk_flags.explanation: Symptom duration is documented only as 'over the past several months,' which is imprecise. Payers may require a specific duration to assess chronicity and appropriateness of surgical intervention.
denial_risk_flags.recommendation: Addend the chart to specify the exact onset date or number of months the patient has experienced left hip pain to strengthen the medical necessity argument.

diagnosis_codes.note: ICD-10 code M16.12 (primary osteoarthritis, left hip) is explicitly documented and is appropriate for CPT 27130.

conservative_treatments_named.note: Only one vaguely described treatment is documented ('over-the-counter pain medication'). No specific drug name, no physical therapy, and no injections are documented. Payers expect at least 3 named modalities.

conservative_treatment_duration.note: Step 1 — Build denominator N: Duration-eligible treatments are: OTC pain medication (1). Single-administration treatments (injections) are excluded; none present. N = 1. Step 2 — N < 2, so score 0 immediately. No further steps needed. 0 of 1 duration-eligible treatments have documented duration. Threshold not met.

imaging_findings.note: No imaging has been completed. X-ray was ordered at today's visit with results pending, and MRI has not been ordered. No imaging findings are available to support medical necessity.

functional_limitations.note: Two specific functional limitations are documented: difficulty ambulating distances greater than 200 feet and inability to perform prolonged standing.

surgical_approach.note: No surgical approach is documented for the left total hip arthroplasty. Anterior, posterior, or lateral approach is not mentioned anywhere in the chart.

cpt_code_valid.note: CPT 27130 (total hip arthroplasty) is explicitly stated in the requested procedure and is consistent with the diagnosis of left hip osteoarthritis (M16.12).

symptom_duration.note: Symptom duration is documented only as 'over the past several months,' which lacks a specific timeframe. Payers require a defined duration to assess chronicity.

hard_blocks.label: Imaging Findings
hard_blocks.message: No imaging results are documented. Payers require objective imaging evidence to authorize surgical intervention.

soft_warnings.label: Surgical Approach
soft_warnings.message: Anatomical approach details strengthen the surgical indication but can be inferred.
